data.title: Affirm, Shopify announce expanded global agreement
data.description:
• Payments company Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ:AFRM) and internet infrastructure services provider Shopify (NYSE:SHOP) (TSX:SHOP:CA) announced on Thursday an expanded global agreement.
  • The renewed partnership enables Affirm to continue as the exclusive pay-over-time provider for Shop Pay Installments in the U.S. and Canada.
  • The partnership also plans to grow into new markets, with the plan to enter the U.K. on the horizon.

data.title: FTAI Aviation soars after review says short seller claims meritless
data.description:
FTAI Aviation (NASDAQ:FTAI) jumped 22% after the aerospace firm said that the allegations made against it by a short seller are without merit.

The Board's Audit Committee has completed its review, conducted by independent legal and forensic accounting advisors, and determined the short seller claims were meritless, the company said in a statement on Thursday. The company expects to file its Form 10-K timely.

"After a thorough and comprehensive review with the support of our independent legal and forensic accounting advisors, we have determined that the assertions made in the short seller reports are unsupported and have no merit," Paul R. Goodwin, Chair of the Audit Committee, said in a statement.

FTAI Aviation (NASDAQ:FTAI) dropped 25% on Jan. 21 after the company's board determined that it will start a review in response to allegations from a Muddy Waters' short report from last month. That report sent FTAI's shares plunging 25% on Jan. 15.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Southern Company beats Q4 top-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
Eagle Point Credit Q4 net investment income misses consensus as yield slips
data.description:
Eagle Point Credit's (NYSE:ECC) Q4 earnings, missing the average analyst estimate, slipped from the prior and year-ago quarters as the effective yield of its collateralized loan obligation ("CLO") portfolio declined. However, the company's management of its portfolio and balance sheet set it up for future performance, the closed-end fund firm said.

Q4 net investment, before non-recurring expenses, was $0.27 per weighted average common share, vs. the $0.28 consensus estimate and down from $0.29 in Q3 and $0.33 in Q4 2023. Q4 2024 net investment, net of $0.03 of non-recurring expenses, amounted to $0.24.

Q4 NII less realized losses of $0.12 per weighted average common share in the current quarter vs. $0.23 of NII less realized losses in the previous quarter and $0.33 of NII and realized gains per weighted average common share in the year-ago quarter.

"During the fourth quarter, we reset 16 and refinanced two of the company’s CLO investments, lengthening the weighted average remaining reinvestment periods of the reset CLOs and lowering the cost of debt financing in all 18 CLOs," said CEO Thomas P. Majewski. "By exercising our majority rights, we are laying the foundation for the portfolio to further enhance its net investment income in 2025 and beyond."

Eagle Point Credit (NYSE:ECC) stock rose 0.2% in premarket trading on Thursday.

The closed-end fund deployed $223.5M in net capital into CLO equity, CLO debt, loan accumulation facilities, and other investments during the quarter. The weighted average yield of new CLO equity investments made by Eagle Point Credit (ECC) during Q4, which includes a provision for credit losses, was 17.8% at the time of investment vs. 18.5% in the prior quarter.

As of Dec. 31, 2024, ECC's weighted effective yield of its CLO equity portfolio (excluding called CLOs), based on amortized cost, was 14.61%, the same as of Sept. 30, and down from 16.70% at Dec. 31, 2023.

Weighted average expected yield of its CLO equity portfolio (excluding called CLOs), based on fair market value, was 19.31% at Dec. 31, 2024, vs. 21.21% at Sept. 30, and 27.10% at Dec. 31, 2023.

Net asset value per common share of $8.38 at Dec. 31, 2024, fell from $8.44 at Sept. 30.

During the quarter, Eagle Point Credit (ECC) issued ~5.2M shares of common stock, ~1.0M shares of 6.75% series D perpetual preferred stock, and 140,574 shares of 8.00% series F term preferred stock under its at-the-market offering program for total net proceeds of ~$71.5M.

It also received total proceeds of $20.3M from issuing convertible perpetual preferred stock.

Conference call at 10:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Eagle Point Credit NII of $0.24 misses by $0.04

data.title: Galaxy Gaming and Pragmatic Play announce five-year licensing agreement extension
data.description:
• Galaxy Gaming (OTCQB:GLXZ) on Thursday announced an agreement with content provider Pragmatic Play to extend their strategic licensing agreement for an additional five years.
  • The agreement expands the integration of Galaxy Gaming's table game content across Pragmatic Play's Live Casino and RNG gaming portfolio, it said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Klaviyo dips despite posting largest quarterly beat since 2023
data.description:
Klaviyo's (NYSE:KVYO) fourth quarter 2024 financial results featured year-over-year revenue growth of 34% to $270.2M, surpassing the consensus estimate, but shares were sinking on Thursday morning.

"Klaviyo reported a solid 4Q, highlighted by its largest revenue upside since its first IPO quarter," said Needham analysts, led by Scott Berg, in a Thursday note. "4Q sales metrics were all positive, including the most net new customer additions (+10k) since the IPO, NRR stabilizing, and Q/Q ARPA growth suggesting positive SMS uptake in a seasonally strong quarter. KVYO shares were mixed after hours, reflecting we believe a negative reaction to the company's new pricing model not driving any upside to FY25 revenue guidance."

Needham maintained its Buy rating on the marketing automation platform and increased its price target to $56 from $46 following the results.

Similarly, KeyBanc Capital Markets reiterated its Overweight rating and boosted its price target to $55 from $45.

"A theme emerging through this earning's season for us is the continued investor preference for small and mid-cap growth," said KeyBanc analysts, led by Jackson Ader, in an investor note. "As the herd of 20%+ growers thins, the scarcity of premium growth has and will put an in-kind premium on those growers' multiples. We believe Klaviyo is adding itself onto the short list of premium growth assets and has a number of tailwinds behind it to sustain its premium growth status."

KeyBanc also noted international growth from Klaviyo last quarter, as it launched in six new languages, with strong enterprise adoption reported in France, Germany and the Nordics.

Finally, Canaccord Genuity maintained its Buy rating and hiked its price target to $50 from $40.

"Looking ahead, at the high-end of guidance, management's initial view pointed to 24% growth and 12% operating margins, which is more or less flat year-over-year and gives Klaviyo leeway to incrementally invest across product category expansion, international growth, and its continued push up-market," said CG analysts, led by David Hynes, in a note.

Competitor HubSpot (HUBS) was down less than 1% on Thursday morning.

data.title: Precision Optics announces pricing of $5.1 million offering
data.description:
• Precision Optics (NASDAQ:POCI) on Thursday said it has entered into securities purchase agreements with institutional and accredited investors for the purchase and sale of 1.27 million shares of the company's common stock pursuant to a registered direct offering at $4.00 per share.
  • The company will receive gross proceeds of about $5.1 million, and expected net proceeds of around $5 million after deducting offering expenses, from the offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay certain indebtedness, expand its facilities to support expected growth and related general working capital purposes.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about February 21, 2025.
  • POCI -2.85% premarket to $4.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nvidia likely to offer up 'strong' results, guidance, despite GB200 NVL constraints: KeyBanc
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is likely to offer up “strong” results and guidance when it reports quarterly results next week, despite the GB200 NVL constraints it has faced, KeyBanc Capital Markets said.

Nvidia shares rose 1% in premarket trading.

“Despite prior concerns regarding constraints associated with the ramp of GB200 NVL servers, we expect NVDA to report strong F4Q results, which we anticipate will solidly beat, and to guide F1Q conservatively and moderately higher than consensus,” KeyBanc analyst John Vinh wrote in a note to clients.

“While we do believe that manufacturing constraints are limiting shipments of GB200 NVL server racks, we believe this will be more than offset by the following: given the lower initial manufacturing yields of GB200 NVL, we believe customers have been able to push out orders of GB200 and backfill with HGX-based B200 servers with x86 head nodes; DeepSeek, as well as limited supply of Huawei's Ascend AI [application specific integrated circuits], has created a surge in demand for H20 GPUs from China [cloud service providers]; we believe NVDA's customers, esp CSPs, are financing its inventory at EMS providers, so effectively sell-in shipments from NVDA to EMS are recognized as revenues.”

In conjunction, Vinh reiterated his Overweight rating and upped his estimates and price target (to $190 from $180) ahead of the results.

Nvidia is slated to report its quarterly results after the close of trading on Feb. 26. A consensus of analysts expect the company to earn $0.61 per share on $26.044B in revenue.

data.title: BridgeBio up 7% as Q4 revenue benefits from acroamidis prescriptions
data.description:
• BridgeBio Pharma (NASDAQ:BBIO) is up ~7% in pre-market trading Thursday after reporting a strong year-over-year revenue gain in Q4 2024 driven in large part to prescriptions of its recently approved drug Attruby (acoramidis).
  • That medicine, approved by the U.S. FDA in late November 2024 for transthyretin amyloid cardiomyopathy (ATTR-CM), has achieved 1,028 unique patient prescriptions written by 516 unique healthcare providers.
  • Revenue of ~$5.9M in the quarter compares to ~$1.7M in the year-ago period.
  • BridgeBio can expect higher acoramidis revenue throughout the year as the drug was approved earlier in February in the EU under the name Beyonttra and approval in Japan is expected in the first half of the year.

data.title: LPL Financial launches platform to streamline the management of alternative investments
data.description:
• Wealth management company LPL Financial Holdings (NASDAQ:LPLA) announced on Thursday the launch of LPL Alts Connect, a platform designed to streamline the way financial advisors and their clients access and manage alternative investments.
  • The platform streamlines the purchasing process and provides tools such as prequalification, e-signature capabilities and the ability to send sales kits directly to clients.
  • LPLA shares were trading 0.19% down pre-market at $382.60.
